content:
FINAL PRODUCTION TASK — FIX PROJECT MEDIA MIXING, RESTORE LANDING REELS, AND CREATE ONE SOURCE OF TRUTH

This is an execution task. Do not provide narrative, assumptions, mock data, placeholders, or screenshots without production evidence.

CURRENT VERIFIED FAILURES

1. Perez Residence displays Casa Rosario or unrelated property pictures.
2. Casa Rosario and Perez Residence are sharing or mixing media.
3. Perez Residence values are inconsistent:
   - Native app screenshot shows approximately 0.0016% minimum ownership.
   - Public landing screenshot shows approximately 0.0020% minimum ownership.
4. Project information is not synchronized between the native app and public landing page.
5. Reels previously visible on the landing page have disappeared.
6. The public landing page, native app, Supabase data, and deployed API are not operating from one canonical project dataset.

OBJECTIVE

Create one canonical production data source for:

- Project identity
- Project slug
- Project pricing
- Investment amount
- ROI
- Fractional minimum
- Minimum ownership percentage
- Developer name
- Location
- Verification badges
- Project gallery
- Cover image
- Reels
- Publication status
- Display order

Both the native Expo app and public landing page must consume the same API/database records.

PHASE 1 — FREEZE AND AUDIT BEFORE CHANGING DATA

Audit the complete data path:

Supabase/database
→ backend API
→ app data service
→ app project cards
→ public landing API
→ landing project cards
→ reels section

Locate and report:

- Exact project table name.
- Exact media/gallery table name.
- Exact reels table or storage source.
- Perez Residence database UUID.
- Casa Rosario database UUID.
- IVX Jacksonville database UUID.
- Slug for every project.
- Every media row linked to each project.
- Every reel row linked to each project.
- Duplicate project IDs.
- Duplicate slugs.
- Duplicate media assignments.
- Orphan media.
- Media rows with null project_id.
- Index-based image mapping.
- Shared fallback images.
- Hardcoded project objects.
- Hardcoded arrays.
- Old local JSON.
- Stale mock data.
- Cache keys that are not project-specific.
- Any query that fetches media without filtering by project_id.
- Any query joining projects and media incorrectly.

Do not modify production records until the incorrect mapping is identified.

PHASE 2 — ENFORCE DATABASE RELATIONSHIPS

Use immutable UUIDs. Never associate media using array index, title text, or display position.

Required canonical relationship:

projects.id
project_media.project_id
project_reels.project_id

Each media item must include:

- id
- project_id
- media_type
- storage_path or public_url
- sort_order
- is_cover
- published
- created_at
- updated_at

Each reel must include:

- id
- project_id
- video_url or storage_path
- thumbnail_url
- caption
- sort_order
- published
- visibility
- created_at
- updated_at

Add or verify foreign keys:

project_media.project_id → projects.id
project_reels.project_id → projects.id

Prevent accidental cross-project media reuse unless explicitly marked as shared.

Add validation:

- Only one cover image per project.
- Every published project must have at least one valid media record.
- Every media record must belong to an existing project.
- Every reel must belong to an existing project or be explicitly marked global.
- Project slugs must be unique.
- Project external IDs must be unique when used.

PHASE 3 — CORRECT PEREZ AND CASA ROSARIO MEDIA

Manually verify every current media row.

Perez Residence must contain only Perez Residence images.

Casa Rosario must contain only Casa Rosario images.

IVX Jacksonville Prime must contain only IVX Jacksonville Prime images.

Remove incorrect associations without deleting valid files from storage.

Reassign media rows to the correct project UUID.

Set the correct cover image for each project.

Verify gallery order using sort_order.

Do not solve this by hiding images or forcing one hardcoded cover image.

PHASE 4 — REMOVE ALL FRONTEND DATA DUPLICATION

Search the entire repository for:

- Perez Residence
- Casa Rosario
- IVX Jacksonville
- projectMedia
- propertyImages
- fallbackImages
- mockProjects
- demoProjects
- sampleProjects
- hardcoded project arrays
- static JSON
- image index mapping
- `projects[index]`
- `media[index]`
- title-based matching
- slug fallback matching

Delete or disable any production fallback that can override database records.

The app and landing page must use the same normalized API response.

Create or use one shared canonical project schema:

{
  id,
  slug,
  title,
  location,
  developer,
  investment_amount,
  sale_price,
  roi_percent,
  fractional_minimum,
  minimum_ownership_percent,
  timeline,
  badges,
  status,
  published,
  display_order,
  cover_image,
  media[],
  reels[]
}

Do not calculate minimum ownership differently in the app and landing page.

Use one backend calculation and return the final value in the API response.

PHASE 5 — RESTORE REELS ON THE PUBLIC LANDING PAGE

Find the last production implementation where reels were visible.

Do not restore stale mock reels.

Connect the landing page to the real published reels source.

The landing page must request:

- Published reels only.
- Active projects only.
- Public visibility only.
- Valid video URLs only.
- Ordered by display_order and created_at.

Required behavior:

- Reels section renders when one or more published reels exist.
- Empty state appears when zero reels exist.
- API errors must show a diagnostic state in development and produce logs in production.
- Reels cannot silently disappear because one media item is invalid.
- One bad reel must not break the full section.
- Video thumbnails must load.
- Video playback must work on Android, iOS, and web.
- Swipe/scroll must not cause cards to jump.
- Reels must preserve aspect ratio.
- No duplicate reels.
- No project media used as reel video unless explicitly configured.

PHASE 6 — SYNCHRONIZE APP AND LANDING PAGE

The following fields must match exactly on both surfaces:

- Project title
- Location
- Cover image
- Gallery images
- Sale price
- Investment amount
- ROI
- Fractional minimum
- Minimum ownership
- Developer
- Badges
- Published status
- Reels

Remove separate calculations and separate project constants.

Both surfaces must consume the same production API version.

Add cache invalidation using:

- project.updated_at
- media.updated_at
- reels.updated_at
- API ETag or version key

Clear any stale CDN, service-worker, React Query, AsyncStorage, Expo, Vercel, Render, and browser caches that are serving obsolete project data.

PHASE 7 — AUTOMATED INTEGRITY TESTS

Create tests that fail deployment when:

1. Two projects receive the same non-shared media ID.
2. Project media is returned without matching project_id.
3. A project slug is duplicated.
4. App and landing API responses differ for the same project.
5. Perez Residence receives Casa Rosario media.
6. Casa Rosario receives Perez Residence media.
7. Minimum ownership differs between app and landing.
8. Published reels exist but the landing API returns zero.
9. A project has no valid cover image.
10. Reels contain invalid video URLs.
11. The landing page silently hides reels after an API failure.
12. Hardcoded production project data is detected.

Add a production integrity endpoint or owner-only diagnostic output showing:

- project count
- media count
- reel count
- media grouped by project
- reels grouped by project
- orphan media count
- orphan reel count
- duplicate slug count
- projects without cover images
- published reels count

PHASE 8 — QA EXACTLY THESE SCREENS

Native app:

- Home
- Featured Properties
- Perez Residence card
- Perez Residence details
- Casa Rosario card
- Casa Rosario details
- IVX Jacksonville card
- Reels screen
- Invest screen
- Market screen

Public landing page:

- Landing page header
- Live JV Deals
- Perez Residence card and gallery
- Casa Rosario card and gallery
- IVX Jacksonville card and gallery
- Reels section
- Details buttons
- Invest Now buttons
- Mobile responsive layout

Test:

- Android physical device
- Android emulator
- iOS layout or simulator
- Mobile browser
- Desktop browser
- Fresh incognito browser
- App after complete restart
- App after cache reset

PHASE 9 — DEPLOYMENT

Commit all corrections to:

ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

Confirm the active production branch and do not deploy from a different Rork-controlled repository.

Deploy the backend and public landing page.

Create a new native development build or Expo update when the app bundle requires it.

Do not claim completion until the production URLs and native app both show the corrected data.

MANDATORY PRODUCTION EVIDENCE

Return only real evidence:

1. Root cause found.
2. File names changed.
3. Database tables changed.
4. Perez Residence project UUID.
5. Casa Rosario project UUID.
6. Correct Perez media IDs.
7. Correct Casa Rosario media IDs.
8. Published reel count before fix.
9. Published reel count after fix.
10. API response for Perez Residence.
11. API response for Casa Rosario.
12. API response for public reels.
13. GitHub repository.
14. Branch.
15. Commit SHA.
16. Render/Vercel deployment ID as applicable.
17. Deployment timestamp.
18. Backend health response.
19. Public production URL.
20. Screenshot of Perez with only Perez images.
21. Screenshot of Casa Rosario with only Casa images.
22. Screenshot of restored landing reels.
23. Screenshot of native app reels.
24. Proof that app and landing values match.
25. Final automated test results.

FINAL STATUS FORMAT

PROJECT_MEDIA_ROOT_CAUSE=
PEREZ_PROJECT_ID=
CASA_ROSARIO_PROJECT_ID=
PEREZ_MEDIA_FIXED=YES/NO
CASA_MEDIA_FIXED=YES/NO
DUPLICATE_SLUGS=
ORPHAN_MEDIA=
PUBLISHED_REELS=
LANDING_REELS_RESTORED=YES/NO
APP_REELS_RESTORED=YES/NO
APP_LANDING_SYNCED=YES/NO
MINIMUM_OWNERSHIP_MATCH=YES/NO
GITHUB_REPO=
BRANCH=
COMMIT_SHA=
BACKEND_DEPLOY_ID=
LANDING_DEPLOY_ID=
HEALTH_STATUS=
PRODUCTION_QA=PASS/FAIL
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Any missing proof must be marked UNVERIFIED. Do not replace evidence with explanation.
